heading: Quarters or other accommodations: to whom furnished
label: 8771 Quarters or other accommodations: to whom furnished
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)7571(a)(less applicability to members on sea duty).34 U.S.C. 911a(less applicability to members on sea duty), .Aug. 2, 1946, ch. 756, § 1560 Stat. 854.5 U.S.C. 421g(b), .Aug. 2, 1946, ch. 756, § 40(b)60 Stat. 8587571(b).34 U.S.C. 911, (4th par.).July 1, 1918, ch. 11440 Stat. 7187571(c).5 U.S.C. 412a, .Aug. 2, 1946, ch. 756, § 3960 Stat. 858In subsection (a) the words “including members of the Nurse Corps” are omitted as surplusage, and the definition of “naval personnel” in , which is applicable to this subsection, is executed.5 U.S.C. 421gIn subsection (b) the words “in any case whether public quarters are available” are substituted for the words “where and when there are no public quarters”. The words “for persons in the Navy and Marine Corps, or serving therewith” are omitted, since these classes of personnel for whom the Secretary makes the determination of availability are the same as those who may be furnished quarters under subsection (a).

children.text: Members of the Coast Guard when it is operating as a service in the Navy.
Members of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration serving with the Navy.

children.label: b
children.text: The Secretary may determine in any case whether public quarters are available within the meaning of any provision of law relating to the assignment of or commutation for public quarters.

children.label: c
children.text: The Secretary, to the extent he considers proper, may delegate the authority conferred by subsection (a), except the authority to prescribe regulations, to any person in the Department of the Navy, with or without authority to make successive redelegations.
